title: At Night
content:
Dark fir-tops foot the moony sky,
        Blue moonlight bars the drive;
        Here at the open window I
        Sit smoking and alive.

        Wind in the branches swells and breaks
        Like ocean on a beach;
        Deep in the sky and my heart there wakes
        A thought I cannot reach.
meaning:
This evocative piece by John Collings Squire, Sir, titled "At Night", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
